title: Xenial Buttery Norwegian Vegetable Casserole
description: A cozy and delicious Norwegian dish featuring a rich buttery sauce and tender vegetables, cooked to perfection in a tandoor, perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the carrots and brussels sprouts with 1/4 cup of melted butter until they are evenly coated.
Spread the vegetables in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast in the tandoor for 20 minutes.
In a medium saucepan, whisk together the flour and milk until smooth.
Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove the sauce from the heat and stir in the shredded cheese until melted and smooth.
Season the sauce with salt and black pepper to taste.
In a large casserole dish, arrange the roasted vegetables in a single layer.
Pour the cheese sauce over the vegetables and smooth the top.
Dot the top of the casserole with the remaining 1/4 cup of melted butter.
Return the casserole to the tandoor and bake for an additional 15 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the sauce is bubbly.
